ingredients.name: pork
ingredients.reason: Swine flesh is explicitly prohibited.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 2:173, 5:3, 6:145, 16:115. Consensus across all schools of Islamic jurisprudence.

ingredients.name: lard
ingredients.reason: Lard is rendered pork fat, prohibited by virtue of its source.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 2:173 (extends to all parts and derivatives of swine; consensus).

ingredients.name: pork gelatin
ingredients.reason: Gelatin extracted from swine, prohibited by source.

ingredients.name: pepsin
ingredients.reason: Pepsin is overwhelmingly sourced from porcine stomach in commercial production. Treat unspecified pepsin as porcine unless the label explicitly states otherwise.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across major halal-certification bodies: unspecified pepsin defaults to porcine origin given industrial supply chains.

ingredients.name: ethanol
ingredients.reason: Intoxicant; prohibited as khamr.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:90-91. Hadith collections (Bukhari, Muslim) on prohibition of khamr. Consensus across all schools.

ingredients.name: alcohol
ingredients.reason: Intoxicant; prohibited as khamr. Trace alcohol from natural fermentation in non-intoxicating quantities is treated separately by some scholars; this rule covers labeled alcohol as an ingredient.

ingredients.name: denatured alcohol
ingredients.reason: Ethanol with added denaturants; the underlying intoxicant remains.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:90-91; methodological consensus that denaturing does not change the haram classification.

ingredients.name: wine
ingredients.reason: Fermented intoxicant; the Prophetic prohibition of khamr names wine specifically.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:90-91; Sahih Muslim 1980 (hadith naming khamr categories); consensus.

ingredients.name: beer
ingredients.reason: Fermented intoxicant.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:90-91; Prophetic ruling: 'every intoxicant is khamr, and every khamr is haram' (Sahih Muslim 2003).

ingredients.name: spirits
ingredients.reason: Distilled intoxicants.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:90-91; Sahih Muslim 2003 ('every intoxicant is khamr').

ingredients.name: blood
ingredients.reason: Flowing blood is explicitly prohibited.

ingredients.name: gelatin
ingredients.reason: Source animal not specified on the label could be porcine, bovine of unspecified slaughter, or fish. Bovine gelatin from non-zabiha-slaughtered cattle is also not consumable. Only an explicitly-certified halal source resolves this.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across IFANCA, HMA UK, JAKIM, MUI: unspecified animal-derived ingredients default to doubtful.

ingredients.name: rennet
ingredients.reason: Microbial and recombinant rennet are halal; animal rennet depends on the slaughter of the source animal. Labels rarely specify.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified rennet origin defaults to doubtful. (Microbial / 'vegetarian' rennet, when explicitly labeled, is halal.)

ingredients.name: mono- and diglycerides of fatty acids
ingredients.reason: Can be derived from animal fat or vegetable oil. Source rarely indicated on labels.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across major halal-cert bodies: unspecified glyceride source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: acetic acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
aliases: acetic acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
ingredients.reason: Inherits the source ambiguity of mono- and diglycerides (animal fat or vegetable oil).
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares glyceride source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: lactic acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
aliases: lactic acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
ingredients.reason: Inherits the source ambiguity of mono- and diglycerides.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares glyceride source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: citric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
aliases: citric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
ingredients.reason: Inherits the source ambiguity of mono- and diglycerides.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares glyceride source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: tartaric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
aliases: tartaric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
ingredients.reason: Inherits the source ambiguity of mono- and diglycerides; also inherits any tartaric-acid wine-origin question.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares glyceride and tartaric-acid source ambiguities.

ingredients.name: datem
aliases: diacetyl tartaric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
ingredients.reason: Inherits the source ambiguity of mono- and diglycerides.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares glyceride source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: mixed esters of mono- and diglycerides
aliases: mixed acetic and tartaric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides
ingredients.reason: Inherits the source ambiguity of mono- and diglycerides.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares glyceride source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: sucrose esters of fatty acids
ingredients.reason: Fatty-acid component can be from animal fat or vegetable oil.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares fatty-acid source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: sucroglycerides
ingredients.reason: Glyceride component shares the standard fat-source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: polyglycerol esters of fatty acids
ingredients.reason: Fatty-acid and glycerol components share the standard fat-source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: polyglycerol polyricinoleate
ingredients.reason: Inherits glycerol-source ambiguity (animal fat or vegetable oil).

ingredients.name: propylene glycol esters of fatty acids
aliases: propylene glycol esters of fatty acids
ingredients.reason: Fatty-acid component can be from animal fat or vegetable oil.

ingredients.name: glycerin
ingredients.reason: Animal-fat-derived glycerin is a common industrial source; vegetable glycerin is also common. Source rarely specified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified glycerin origin defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: lecithin
ingredients.reason: Soy and sunflower lecithin are halal; egg lecithin is halal; animal-derived lecithin is doubtful. When the label says only 'lecithin', the source is unverified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified emulsifier source defaults to doubtful. Explicitly-labeled soy/sunflower lecithin is halal.

ingredients.name: natural flavors
ingredients.reason: An umbrella term that may include alcohol-based extractants, animal-derived components, or castoreum. Not verifiable from the label.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: 'natural flavors' is non-specific by regulatory definition (US FDA 21 CFR 101.22) and defaults to doubtful for halal purposes.

ingredients.name: flavor enhancer
ingredients.reason: Generic label without specifying which enhancer. Some specific enhancers (E631, E635) carry source concerns.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified enhancer defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: carmine
ingredients.reason: Insect-derived (cochineal). Scholarly disagreement: some authorities (e.g. IFANCA) treat it as permissible; others (notably some Hanafi rulings) treat insect-derived colorants as not permissible.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement between certification bodies; defaults to doubtful pending the user's chosen authority.

ingredients.name: shellac
ingredients.reason: Resin secreted by the lac insect. Same scholarly disagreement as carmine.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on insect-derived ingredients; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: vanilla extract
ingredients.reason: Standard vanilla extract is prepared in an ethanol carrier (typically 35%+ alcohol by US FDA definition). Scholarly disagreement on whether the alcohol carrier renders the final product haram or whether the small final-product concentration is permissible.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful. Vanilla bean and alcohol-free vanilla flavoring, when explicitly labeled, are halal.

ingredients.name: l-cysteine
ingredients.reason: Industrial L-cysteine is sourced from human hair, duck/poultry feathers, or microbial fermentation. Source rarely specified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: human-hair derived L-cysteine is rejected by many halal authorities; non-zabiha poultry source also doubtful; microbial source is halal. Defaults to doubtful when unspecified.

ingredients.name: enzymes
ingredients.reason: Generic 'enzymes' label gives no source information. Microbial enzymes are halal; animal-derived enzymes depend on the source animal's slaughter.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified enzyme source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: lipase
ingredients.reason: Lipase is sourced from microbial fermentation, plant material, or animal pancreatic tissue. Source rarely specified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified enzyme source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: trypsin
ingredients.reason: Trypsin is typically sourced from animal pancreas. Microbial trypsin exists but is uncommon in food applications.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: animal-derived enzyme of unspecified slaughter defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: whey
ingredients.reason: Whey is a by-product of cheese making. If the cheese was set with animal rennet from non-zabiha-slaughtered animals, the whey inherits the doubt. Today microbial rennet is common but not universal, and labels rarely indicate which.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified rennet origin in upstream production defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: casein
ingredients.reason: Same upstream rennet question as whey when produced via cheese-making routes.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares the rennet-origin doubt.

ingredients.name: cheese
ingredients.reason: Most commercial cheese is set with rennet. Microbial/recombinant rennet is halal; animal rennet depends on slaughter. Labels rarely specify.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified rennet origin defaults to doubtful. Cheese explicitly labeled with microbial / vegetarian rennet is halal.

ingredients.name: tallow
ingredients.reason: Tallow is rendered animal fat (typically beef or sheep). Halal status depends on whether the source animal was zabiha-slaughtered, which is rarely indicated on a non-certified product.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified slaughter defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: suet
ingredients.reason: Hard fat from around the kidneys/loins of beef or mutton. Same slaughter-origin question as tallow.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares slaughter-origin doubt with tallow.

ingredients.name: animal fat
ingredients.reason: Generic label gives no information on species or slaughter.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-source ingredient defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: bone phosphate
ingredients.reason: Derived from animal bones; the slaughter-origin question applies.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: stearic acid
ingredients.reason: Can be derived from animal fat or vegetable oil. Source rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fatty-acid source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: magnesium stearate
ingredients.reason: Magnesium salt of stearic acid; inherits the source ambiguity of stearic acid.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares stearic-acid source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: sodium stearoyl lactylate
ingredients.reason: Inherits stearic-acid source ambiguity.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares stearic-acid source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: calcium stearoyl lactylate
ingredients.reason: Inherits stearic-acid source ambiguity.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares stearic-acid source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: emulsifier
ingredients.reason: Generic label without specifying which emulsifier. Many emulsifiers are halal; some are doubtful (E471, E472a-f, E481, E482, E542, E570 family).
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified emulsifier defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: tartaric acid
ingredients.reason: Tartaric acid is historically a wine-industry by-product (recovered from tartar deposits in winemaking). Synthetic tartaric acid is also produced. Some authorities consider wine-derived tartaric acid doubtful by association even after purification.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on wine-industry-derived ingredients; defaults to doubtful pending source clarification.

ingredients.name: wine vinegar
ingredients.reason: Produced by acetic-acid fermentation of wine. The majority position (transformation / istihalah) treats the resulting vinegar as halal; a minority view maintains the prohibition by association.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on wine-vinegar transformation; defaults to doubtful to honor the disagreement.

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.reason: Traditionally brewed soy sauce can develop 1-3% naturally-fermented alcohol. Some authorities (e.g. JAKIM) treat this as permissible since the alcohol is a fermentation by-product, not deliberately added; others mark it doubtful or haram.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful. Explicitly halal-certified soy sauce is halal.

ingredients.name: disodium inosinate
ingredients.reason: Commonly produced from fish (sardines) or animal tissue, including porcine sources, though microbial production exists. Source rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-source ingredient defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: disodium 5'-ribonucleotides
ingredients.reason: Mixture of E627 (disodium guanylate) and E631 (disodium inosinate). Inherits E631's source-ambiguity concern.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares E631 source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.reason: Universally permissible.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus; pre-Islamic and Islamic legal continuity treats water as the default halal substance.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.reason: Mineral; no animal or intoxicant origin.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: minerals without animal-source processing are halal by default.

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived (sugarcane or sugar beet). Note: bone-char filtration is sometimes raised as a concern, but the prevailing position across major halal-cert bodies is that the final sugar is halal because no animal substance remains.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across IFANCA, HMA UK, JAKIM that bone-char-filtered sugar is halal in the final product.

ingredients.name: honey
ingredients.reason: Universally permissible; affirmed by Quranic reference.

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived from peppercorns.

ingredients.name: cumin
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: coriander
ingredients.reason: Spice/herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: cardamom
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: cloves
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: nutmeg
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: allspice
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: bay leaves
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: paprika
ingredients.reason: Spice / colorant; plant-derived from dried Capsicum peppers.

ingredients.name: chili
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: cinnamon
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: turmeric
ingredients.reason: Spice / colorant; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: ginger
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived.

ingredients.name: mustard
ingredients.reason: Spice / condiment; plant-derived. (Some prepared mustards include wine vinegar — those would inherit the wine-vinegar question.)
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus on mustard seed itself; check prepared-mustard ingredients separately.

ingredients.name: fennel
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: basil
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: oregano
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: thyme
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: rosemary
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: sage
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: parsley
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: mint
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived. (Mint extract in alcohol carrier would inherit the vanilla-extract question — most are oil-based.)
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus on mint herb; check extract carrier separately.

ingredients.name: dill
ingredients.reason: Herb; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: anise
ingredients.reason: Spice; plant-derived.

ingredients.name: citric acid
ingredients.reason: Industrially produced via microbial fermentation (Aspergillus niger) on sugar substrate. No animal or intoxicant component.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: microbial-fermentation acids on plant substrate are halal.

ingredients.name: ascorbic acid
ingredients.reason: Synthesized industrially from glucose. No animal or intoxicant component.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: synthetic vitamin from plant-substrate process is halal.

ingredients.name: acetic acid
ingredients.reason: The acid component of vinegar. Industrially produced by synthesis or fermentation on plant substrates.

ingredients.name: lactic acid
ingredients.reason: Today commercially produced via microbial fermentation on plant substrates (corn, beet sugar). Despite the name, modern food-grade lactic acid is not derived from milk.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: microbial fermentation on plant substrate is halal.

ingredients.name: malic acid
ingredients.reason: Industrially synthesized; also occurs naturally in apples and other fruits.

ingredients.name: fumaric acid
ingredients.reason: Industrially synthesized.

ingredients.name: phosphoric acid
ingredients.reason: Mineral acid; produced from phosphate rock.

ingredients.name: potassium sorbate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative; no animal or intoxicant content.

ingredients.name: sorbic acid
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative.

ingredients.name: sodium benzoate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative.

ingredients.name: calcium propionate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative; calcium salt of propionic acid.

ingredients.name: sulfur dioxide
ingredients.reason: Mineral preservative; no animal or intoxicant content.

ingredients.name: stevia
ingredients.reason: Sweetener extracted from the Stevia rebaudiana plant.

ingredients.name: sucralose
ingredients.reason: Synthetic sweetener derived from sugar.

ingredients.name: aspartame
ingredients.reason: Synthetic dipeptide sweetener.

ingredients.name: saccharin
ingredients.reason: Synthetic sweetener.

ingredients.name: acesulfame potassium
ingredients.reason: Synthetic sweetener.

ingredients.name: erythritol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol produced by microbial fermentation on plant-substrate sugars.

ingredients.name: xylitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol produced from plant sources (typically birch or corn).

ingredients.name: maltitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol derived from maltose (plant-derived).

ingredients.name: sorbitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol synthesized from glucose.

ingredients.name: xanthan gum
ingredients.reason: Polysaccharide produced by microbial fermentation (Xanthomonas campestris) on plant-substrate sugars.

ingredients.name: guar gum
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from guar bean.

ingredients.name: locust bean gum
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from carob seeds.

ingredients.name: agar
ingredients.reason: Seaweed-derived gelling agent.

ingredients.name: carrageenan
ingredients.reason: Seaweed-derived thickener.

ingredients.name: pectin
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; extracted from fruit (typically citrus or apple).

ingredients.name: gum arabic
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; sap from Acacia trees.

ingredients.name: modified food starch
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived (typically corn, tapioca, potato, or wheat); the chemical or physical modifications do not introduce animal content.

ingredients.name: wheat flour
ingredients.reason: Milled wheat. (Some bleaching agents have been historically debated, but the flour itself is halal.)

ingredients.name: corn starch
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; extracted from corn endosperm.

ingredients.name: tapioca starch
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from cassava root.

ingredients.name: potato starch
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from potato.

ingredients.name: rice flour
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; milled rice.

ingredients.name: arrowroot
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived starch; from Maranta plant rhizomes.

ingredients.name: corn syrup
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; produced enzymatically from corn starch (the enzymes used industrially are microbial).
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across major halal-cert bodies.

ingredients.name: dextrose
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived sugar; typically from corn or wheat.

ingredients.name: fructose
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived sugar.

ingredients.name: maltodextrin
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived (typically corn, rice, or potato starch). Microbial enzymes used in production are halal.

ingredients.name: molasses
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; sugar-refining by-product.

ingredients.name: milk
ingredients.reason: Milk from a halal-permitted animal (cow, buffalo, sheep, goat) is halal.

ingredients.name: cream
ingredients.reason: Dairy fat from milk.

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.reason: Dairy product from milk.

ingredients.name: yogurt
ingredients.reason: Cultured dairy product. (Cultures used are microbial.)

ingredients.name: egg
ingredients.reason: Eggs from halal-permitted birds (chicken, duck, quail, etc.) are halal.

ingredients.name: egg white
ingredients.reason: Halal as part of the egg.

ingredients.name: egg yolk
ingredients.reason: Halal as part of the egg.

ingredients.name: coffee
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; roasted coffee beans.

ingredients.name: tea
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from Camellia sinensis.

ingredients.name: vinegar
ingredients.reason: Acetic-acid solution. The transformation (istihalah) of any source material into vinegar is the majority position for halal status. Wine vinegar is treated separately.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus on the transformation principle for non-wine vinegars.

ingredients.name: vegetable oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived. (Hydrogenated and partially-hydrogenated forms are still plant-derived; the hydrogenation process does not add animal content.)

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; pressed from olives.

ingredients.name: sunflower oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; pressed from sunflower seeds.

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; pressed from coconut.

ingredients.name: palm oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from oil palm fruit.

ingredients.name: peanut oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; pressed from peanuts.

ingredients.name: sesame oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; pressed from sesame seeds.

ingredients.name: avocado oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from avocado fruit.

ingredients.name: cocoa
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from cacao bean.

ingredients.name: yeast
ingredients.reason: Single-cell fungus. Permissible; not classed with intoxicants.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across major halal-cert bodies that culinary yeast is halal.

ingredients.name: sodium bicarbonate
ingredients.reason: Mineral compound; no animal source.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: minerals are halal by default.

ingredients.name: soy protein
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from soybeans.

ingredients.name: wheat protein
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from wheat.

ingredients.name: pea protein
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived; from yellow peas.

ingredients.name: calcium carbonate
ingredients.reason: Mineral compound.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: minerals are halal by default.

ingredients.name: iron
ingredients.reason: Mineral / mineral salt.

ingredients.name: niacin
ingredients.reason: Synthetic vitamin.

ingredients.name: vitamin a
ingredients.reason: Industrially synthesized retinol or plant-derived beta-carotene precursor; both halal.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus on synthetic and plant-derived forms.

ingredients.name: vitamin d
ingredients.reason: Vitamin D3 is most commonly produced from lanolin (sheep wool grease, considered halal as wool is permissible regardless of slaughter) or from fish liver oil. Vitamin D2 is produced from yeast/plant sources. A minority view questions lanolin from non-zabiha sheep.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across major halal-cert bodies that lanolin-derived vitamin D3 is halal.

ingredients.name: vitamin e
ingredients.reason: Industrially derived from vegetable-oil distillates (typically soybean or sunflower).

ingredients.name: monosodium glutamate
ingredients.reason: Today produced industrially via microbial fermentation on plant-substrate sugars (typically sugarcane molasses or starch).
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus on modern microbial-fermentation production. Historic concerns about animal-fat substrate are not applicable to modern production.

ingredients.name: disodium guanylate
ingredients.reason: Typically produced from yeast extract via microbial fermentation. (Often paired with E631; check separately.)

ingredients.name: caramel color
ingredients.reason: Produced by controlled heating of plant-derived sugars. Class III (E150c) and Class IV (E150d) involve ammonium compounds in processing, but no animal content.

ingredients.name: titanium dioxide
ingredients.reason: Mineral colorant.

ingredients.name: annatto
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived colorant from Bixa orellana seeds.

ingredients.name: beta-carotene
ingredients.reason: Synthetic or plant-derived (carrot, algae) provitamin A colorant.

ingredients.name: anthocyanins
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived colorant from grape skins, berries, etc.

ingredients.name: riboflavin
ingredients.reason: Synthetic or microbial-fermentation-produced vitamin / yellow colorant.

ingredients.name: tartrazine
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye; no animal or intoxicant content.

ingredients.name: sunset yellow
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye.

ingredients.name: allura red
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye.

ingredients.name: brilliant blue
ingredients.reason: Synthetic dye.

ingredients.name: beef
ingredients.reason: Beef is halal in principle but only when the source animal was zabiha-slaughtered (the name of God invoked, throat severed, blood drained). Commercial beef without halal certification is rarely zabiha.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:3 (slaughter conditions); 6:118-121 (name of God to be pronounced at slaughter). Methodological consensus across major halal-cert bodies: unspecified slaughter defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: veal
ingredients.reason: Calf meat — same zabiha-slaughter requirement as beef.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:3; 6:118-121. Methodological consensus: unspecified slaughter defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: chicken
ingredients.reason: Chicken is halal in principle but requires zabiha slaughter. Commercial chicken without halal certification is typically mechanically slaughtered or electrically stunned, which several authorities treat as doubtful.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:3; 6:118-121. Methodological consensus: unspecified poultry slaughter defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: turkey
ingredients.reason: Halal in principle but requires zabiha slaughter. 'Turkey bacon' is bacon-shaped turkey, not pork — but inherits the slaughter question.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:3; 6:118-121. Methodological consensus: unspecified poultry slaughter defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: duck
ingredients.reason: Duck flesh halal in principle; zabiha slaughter required. Foie gras inherits the same slaughter question.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:3; 6:118-121. Methodological consensus: unspecified poultry slaughter defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: goose
ingredients.reason: Halal in principle; zabiha slaughter required.

ingredients.name: lamb
ingredients.reason: Halal in principle; zabiha slaughter required.

ingredients.name: mutton
ingredients.reason: Adult sheep meat — same zabiha requirement as lamb.

ingredients.name: goat
ingredients.reason: Halal in principle; zabiha slaughter required.

ingredients.name: venison
ingredients.reason: Wild herbivores are halal in principle. Hunted game is halal if Allah's name was invoked at the kill; commercially-sold venison rarely indicates this. Farm-raised venison must be zabiha-slaughtered.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:4 (hunted prey lawful when caught for you and Allah's name invoked); 5:3. Methodological consensus.

ingredients.name: rabbit
ingredients.reason: Rabbit meat is generally permissible per the majority position (the Prophet ate rabbit per Sahih Bukhari 5535), but the Maliki and some Hanafi opinions disliked it; commercial supply rarely indicates zabiha slaughter.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Bukhari 5535. Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful pending the user's authority and slaughter information.

ingredients.name: horse meat
ingredients.reason: Documented school-level disagreement: Hanafi treats horse meat as makruh tahrimi (close to haram); Shafi'i, Hanbali, and the dominant view of Maliki permit it when zabiha-slaughtered. Defaults to doubtful pending the user's authority.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Bukhari 5520, Sahih Muslim 1941 (permission of horse meat in some narrations). Hanafi position from later jurists. Documented school-level disagreement.

ingredients.name: donkey meat
ingredients.reason: Domestic donkey flesh is prohibited by explicit prophetic ruling. Mule (donkey-horse hybrid) inherits the prohibition.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Bukhari 4218, Sahih Muslim 1936 (prohibition of domestic donkey flesh on the day of Khaybar). Consensus.

ingredients.name: carnivorous mammal meat
ingredients.reason: Mammals with fangs/canines that prey on other animals are prohibited.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Muslim 1933 (Prophet prohibited every fanged beast of prey). Consensus across all schools.

ingredients.name: bird of prey meat
ingredients.reason: Birds with talons that hunt other animals are prohibited.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Muslim 1934 (prohibition of every bird with talons). Consensus.

ingredients.name: frog
ingredients.reason: Prophetic prohibition against killing frogs is extended by the majority of jurists to consuming them.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sunan Abu Dawud 3871, al-Nasa'i 4355 (Prophet forbade killing the frog). Hanafi, Shafi'i (per al-Nawawi), and Hanbali positions hold this haram.

ingredients.name: snake meat
ingredients.reason: Snakes are classified among prohibited animals across all major schools.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: predatory and from the prohibited-fanged-beast category by extension.

ingredients.name: crocodile
ingredients.reason: Predatory carnivores with fangs.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Muslim 1933 (prohibition of every fanged beast of prey).

ingredients.name: turtle
ingredients.reason: Documented school-level disagreement: Hanafi treats land tortoise as haram; Maliki and Shafi'i schools generally permit sea-turtle if it lives only in water and is slaughtered.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: insect
ingredients.reason: Insects in general are not among the lawful foods per the majority. The notable exception is locust, which has its own rule.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across Hanafi, Shafi'i, and Hanbali. (Maliki view permits insects under some conditions.) Default classification reflects the majority.

ingredients.name: locust
ingredients.reason: Locust is explicitly lawful by prophetic ruling — one of two carrions (along with fish) declared permissible without slaughter.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sunan Ibn Majah 3218 ('We have been allowed two carrions and two bloods: fish and locust...'). Consensus.

ingredients.name: fish
ingredients.reason: Sea creatures with scales are halal across all schools. Slaughter requirements do not apply to fish; even fish that die of themselves in water are permissible.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:96 ('Lawful to you is what you catch from the sea'). Sunan Ibn Majah 3218 (fish among the permitted carrions). Consensus.

ingredients.name: shark
ingredients.reason: Shark is a fish; classed halal across the majority. The Hanafi school treats only true 'fish' (those with scales) as halal — sharks have placoid scales which most modern Hanafi scholars accept.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:96. Methodological position with documented school-level nuance; default halal.

ingredients.name: eel
ingredients.reason: Documented school-level disagreement on whether eel qualifies as 'fish with scales' for Hanafi purposes. Shafi'i, Maliki, and Hanbali treat all sea creatures as permissible; Hanafi requires scales.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful pending the user's chosen authority.

ingredients.name: shrimp
ingredients.reason: Documented school-level disagreement: Maliki, Shafi'i, and Hanbali treat shrimp as halal sea creature; classical Hanafi holds shrimp as not 'true fish' and rules it makruh tahrimi or haram. Modern Hanafi institutional rulings (e.g. Deoband) generally permit shrimp.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful pending the user's chosen authority.

ingredients.name: crab
ingredients.reason: Same school-level disagreement as shrimp: permissible per three Sunni schools, classically treated as not-fish under Hanafi. Surimi is fish-paste (which is fish), but commercial 'imitation crab' may include flavorings of unspecified origin.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: lobster
ingredients.reason: Same school-level disagreement as shrimp/crab.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: oyster
ingredients.reason: Bivalve mollusk. Shafi'i, Maliki, Hanbali permit; Hanafi treats as not-fish and rules doubtful.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: clam
ingredients.reason: Bivalve mollusk; same school disagreement as oyster.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: mussel
ingredients.reason: Bivalve mollusk; same school disagreement as oyster.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: scallop
ingredients.reason: Bivalve mollusk; same school disagreement as oyster.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: octopus
ingredients.reason: Cephalopod; same Hanafi-vs-three-schools disagreement as the other non-scaled sea creatures.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: squid
ingredients.reason: Cephalopod; same school-level disagreement.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: cuttlefish
ingredients.reason: Cephalopod; same school-level disagreement.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: snail
ingredients.reason: Land snails are not among the permissible animal categories per the majority of schools. (Sea snails fall into the broader sea-creature debate and are doubtful for Hanafi adherents.)
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across Hanafi, Shafi'i, and Hanbali for land snails.

ingredients.name: halal certified
ingredients.reason: Explicit halal certification or zabiha-slaughter attestation. The certifying body should still be evaluated for trustworthiness, but the term itself is a positive marker.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: explicit halal certification or zabiha-slaughter language is a positive marker.

ingredients.name: non-zabihah
ingredients.reason: Mechanical, electrical, captive-bolt, or otherwise pre-stunned slaughter is treated as not meeting zabiha conditions by some authorities (notably Hanafi/Shafi'i traditional rulings and bodies like JAKIM); other authorities (e.g. some HMA UK rulings) permit reversible stunning followed by manual cut.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented disagreement among certification bodies on stunning. Defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: broth
ingredients.reason: Stock and broth source-animal slaughter is rarely indicated; bouillon cubes commonly include hydrolyzed proteins, fats, and 'natural flavors' from unspecified animal origin.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-origin ingredient defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: shortening
ingredients.reason: Historically often lard-based; modern shortening is usually fully hydrogenated vegetable oil but commercial labels are inconsistent. 'All-vegetable' is halal in principle; 'shortening' alone is unverified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fat source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: marshmallow
ingredients.reason: Standard marshmallows use gelatin of unspecified animal origin. Vegan/kosher marshmallows use agar or pectin and are halal; unspecified marshmallows inherit gelatin's source ambiguity.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares unspecified gelatin's source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: marzipan
ingredients.reason: Often contains alcohol-based flavor extracts (rum, kirsch). Alcohol-free formulations exist.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified flavoring carrier defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: edible fat
ingredients.reason: Generic 'edible fat' label gives no source information.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fat source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: glycerol monostearate
ingredients.reason: Inherits stearic-acid / glycerin source ambiguity (animal vs. vegetable).
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares stearic-acid source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: polysorbate
ingredients.reason: Polysorbates are esters of fatty acids on a sorbitol/sorbitan backbone. The fatty-acid component can be plant or animal in origin; rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fatty-acid source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: sorbitan ester
ingredients.reason: Same fatty-acid source ambiguity as polysorbate.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares the fatty-acid source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: sodium nitrite
ingredients.reason: Inorganic salt used as cure/colorant in meat. Synthetic; no animal origin. The cured meat itself may be a separate concern.

ingredients.name: potassium nitrite
ingredients.reason: Inorganic salt; synthetic.

ingredients.name: sodium nitrate
ingredients.reason: Inorganic salt; synthetic.

ingredients.name: potassium nitrate
ingredients.reason: Inorganic salt; synthetic.

ingredients.name: propionic acid
ingredients.reason: Synthetic short-chain fatty acid; preservative.

ingredients.name: sodium propionate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative salt.

ingredients.name: bha
ingredients.reason: Synthetic phenolic antioxidant.

ingredients.name: bht
ingredients.reason: Synthetic phenolic antioxidant.

ingredients.name: tbhq
ingredients.reason: Synthetic phenolic antioxidant.

ingredients.name: propyl gallate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic ester of gallic acid; antioxidant.

ingredients.name: tocopherols
ingredients.reason: Vitamin E and its variants. Plant-derived in food applications.

ingredients.name: sodium alginate
ingredients.reason: Brown-seaweed extract.

ingredients.name: potassium alginate
ingredients.reason: Brown-seaweed extract salt.

ingredients.name: calcium alginate
ingredients.reason: Brown-seaweed extract salt.

ingredients.name: propylene glycol alginate
ingredients.reason: Esterified alginate; synthetic on a seaweed-derived backbone.

ingredients.name: cellulose
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived structural polysaccharide.

ingredients.name: methylcellulose
ingredients.reason: Modified plant cellulose.

ingredients.name: ethyl cellulose
ingredients.reason: Modified plant cellulose.

ingredients.name: hydroxypropyl methylcellulose
ingredients.reason: Modified plant cellulose.

ingredients.name: carboxymethyl cellulose
ingredients.reason: Modified plant cellulose.

ingredients.name: silicon dioxide
ingredients.reason: Mineral; anti-caking agent.

ingredients.name: talc
ingredients.reason: Mineral; anti-caking.

ingredients.name: magnesium silicate
ingredients.reason: Mineral; anti-caking.

ingredients.name: calcium silicate
ingredients.reason: Mineral; anti-caking.

ingredients.name: beeswax
ingredients.reason: Wax produced by honeybees. Same scholarly basis as honey: bee products are permissible.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus by analogy to honey (Quran 16:68-69).

ingredients.name: carnauba wax
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived wax from Brazilian palm leaves.

ingredients.name: candelilla wax
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived wax from candelilla shrub.

ingredients.name: microcrystalline wax
ingredients.reason: Petroleum-derived wax.

ingredients.name: mannitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol; synthetic or fermented from corn/seaweed.

ingredients.name: isomalt
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol derived from beet sugar.

ingredients.name: lactitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol derived from lactose, which is sourced from milk whey. Inherits whey's upstream rennet ambiguity.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares whey's source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: thaumatin
ingredients.reason: Sweet protein extracted from katemfe fruit.

ingredients.name: neotame
ingredients.reason: Synthetic high-intensity sweetener.

ingredients.name: cyclamate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic sweetener.

ingredients.name: neohesperidine dc
ingredients.reason: Synthetic citrus-derived sweetener.

ingredients.name: iron oxide
ingredients.reason: Mineral colorant.

ingredients.name: amaranth (color)
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye (no relation to the amaranth grain).

ingredients.name: ponceau 4r
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye despite the historical name; not derived from cochineal insects.

ingredients.name: erythrosine
ingredients.reason: Synthetic xanthene dye.

ingredients.name: patent blue v
ingredients.reason: Synthetic triphenylmethane dye.

ingredients.name: indigo carmine
ingredients.reason: Synthetic indigoid dye.

ingredients.name: quinoline yellow
ingredients.reason: Synthetic dye.

ingredients.name: carmoisine
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye.

ingredients.name: lutein
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived xanthophyll pigment (typically marigold).

ingredients.name: lycopene
ingredients.reason: Plant carotenoid (typically tomato).

ingredients.name: paprika extract
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived colorant.

ingredients.name: chlorophyll
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived pigment.

ingredients.name: beetroot red
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived pigment from beetroot.

ingredients.name: vegetable carbon
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived (charred plant material).

ingredients.name: carbon dioxide
ingredients.reason: Inorganic gas.

ingredients.name: potassium chloride
ingredients.reason: Inorganic salt.

ingredients.name: ammonium bicarbonate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic raising agent.

ingredients.name: potassium carbonate
ingredients.reason: Inorganic salt.

ingredients.name: calcium phosphate
ingredients.reason: Calcium phosphates are made from mineral rock phosphate (halal) or from animal bones (e542 — see bone phosphate). When labeled simply as 'calcium phosphate' without source, the origin is ambiguous.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified phosphate source defaults to doubtful when an animal-derived alternative exists in commerce.

ingredients.name: sodium phosphate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic; mineral-derived.

ingredients.name: potassium phosphate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic; mineral-derived.

ingredients.name: sodium pyrophosphate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic phosphate salt.

ingredients.name: sodium tripolyphosphate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic phosphate salt.

ingredients.name: tragacanth
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived gum.

ingredients.name: karaya gum
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived gum.

ingredients.name: tara gum
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived gum.

ingredients.name: gellan gum
ingredients.reason: Microbially-fermented polysaccharide.

ingredients.name: glycine
ingredients.reason: Amino acid produced synthetically or by hydrolysis of animal/plant proteins. Source rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified amino-acid source defaults to doubtful when an animal-derived alternative is in commerce.

ingredients.name: dimethylpolysiloxane
ingredients.reason: Synthetic silicone anti-foaming agent.

ingredients.name: starch
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived polysaccharide.

ingredients.name: lactose
ingredients.reason: Lactose is sourced from milk whey. Inherits whey's upstream rennet-origin ambiguity.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares whey's source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: pancreatin
ingredients.reason: Pancreatic enzyme preparation typically extracted from porcine or bovine pancreas. Source rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-derived enzyme defaults to doubtful; porcine pancreatin is haram.

ingredients.name: chocolate
ingredients.reason: Standard chocolate commonly contains soy lecithin (typically halal but unspecified), milk solids (whey-rennet ambiguity), and may include alcohol-based flavorings (vanilla extract, liqueurs). 'Chocolate liquor' is not alcohol — it is the liquid form of cocoa solids — but the bar's other ingredients drive the doubt.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: composite product inheriting upstream ambiguity (lecithin, dairy whey, vanilla flavoring carrier).

ingredients.name: cocoa butter
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived fat extracted from cocoa beans.

ingredients.name: cocoa powder
ingredients.reason: Pulverized cocoa solids from defatted cocoa beans.

ingredients.name: collagen
ingredients.reason: Collagen is sourced from animal connective tissue — bovine hide, porcine skin, fish, or chicken. Inherits the same source ambiguity as gelatin.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-derived ingredient defaults to doubtful; porcine collagen is haram.

ingredients.name: chondroitin
ingredients.reason: Sourced from animal cartilage — typically bovine, porcine, or shark. Source rarely indicated on supplement labels.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal source defaults to doubtful; porcine chondroitin is haram.

ingredients.name: glucosamine
ingredients.reason: Most commercial glucosamine is extracted from shellfish exoskeletons (shrimp, crab, lobster), inheriting the same Hanafi-vs-three-schools disagreement as those creatures. Microbially-fermented (vegetarian) glucosamine is halal when explicitly labeled.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement on shellfish source; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: chitin
ingredients.reason: Sourced from crustacean (shrimp/crab) shells in commercial production. Inherits the shellfish school-level disagreement; fungal chitosan is halal when explicitly labeled.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement on shellfish source; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: caviar
ingredients.reason: Fish eggs are part of the fish; sea creatures with scales are halal across all schools, and roe inherits the parent fish's status.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Quran 5:96. Methodological consensus by extension from the fish ruling.

ingredients.name: kombucha
ingredients.reason: Fermented tea that develops measurable ethanol (typically 0.5-3% in commercial bottles, higher in homebrew). Some authorities apply the soy-sauce reasoning (natural fermentation by-product); others treat any meaningful ethanol content as haram.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on naturally-fermented foods; defaults to doubtful. Halal-certified kombucha (typically reformulated to <0.1% alcohol) is halal.

ingredients.name: kefir
ingredients.reason: Fermented dairy beverage that develops trace alcohol (typically 0.5-2% in homemade, lower commercial). Same naturally-fermented disagreement as kombucha and soy sauce. Milk kefir also inherits whey-rennet ambiguity.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on naturally-fermented foods; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: miso
ingredients.reason: Fermented soybean paste. Some commercial miso has alcohol added as a preservative; even alcohol-free miso develops trace ethanol from fermentation. Same naturally-fermented disagreement as soy sauce.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful. Halal-certified miso is halal.

ingredients.name: hydrolyzed vegetable protein
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived protein broken down by acid or enzymatic hydrolysis. The 'vegetable' qualifier resolves the source.

ingredients.name: hydrolyzed protein
ingredients.reason: Protein source unspecified. Animal-derived hydrolysates inherit the slaughter-origin question; porcine hydrolysates are haram. Plant-source variants exist but require an explicit 'vegetable' qualifier.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-derived ingredient defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: inulin
ingredients.reason: Plant-derived prebiotic fiber, typically from chicory root or agave.

ingredients.name: vanillin
ingredients.reason: Synthetic or wood-derived vanilla flavor compound. Distinct from vanilla extract — vanillin alone is the isolated compound, typically supplied as a powder with no alcohol carrier.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus. (See separate vanilla-extract rule for the alcohol-carrier concern.)

ingredients.name: taurine
ingredients.reason: Originally isolated from ox bile (the Latin name reflects this), historically also extracted from animal tissues. Most modern commercial taurine in food and energy drinks is synthetic, but labels rarely indicate.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified source where an animal-derived industrial supply exists defaults to doubtful. Synthetically-produced taurine, when explicitly labeled, is halal.

ingredients.name: royal jelly
ingredients.reason: Bee secretion; same scholarly basis as honey — bee products are permissible.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus by analogy to honey (Quran 16:68-69).

ingredients.name: propolis
ingredients.reason: Resinous bee product; same scholarly basis as honey.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus by analogy to honey (Quran 16:68-69).

ingredients.name: nutritional yeast
ingredients.reason: Deactivated yeast; no fermentation alcohol concern since the yeast is inactive when packaged.

ingredients.name: spirulina
ingredients.reason: Cyanobacterium / algae; sea/water-plant by classification.

ingredients.name: caramel flavoring
ingredients.reason: Distinct from caramel color (E150). Liquid caramel flavorings often use ethanol as a carrier solvent — same vanilla-extract disagreement on the alcohol carrier.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on alcohol carriers in flavorings; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: modified milk ingredients
ingredients.reason: Canadian/regulatory umbrella term covering whey, casein, lactose, milk protein concentrate, etc. — any of which inherit the upstream rennet-origin ambiguity.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares the unspecified-rennet ambiguity of its constituents.

ingredients.name: yeast extract
ingredients.reason: Deactivated yeast cell contents used as a flavor ingredient. The autolysis process inactivates the yeast and does not produce ethanol.

ingredients.name: bat meat
ingredients.reason: Bats fall outside lawful animal categories per the majority of jurists; prophetic narrations also forbid killing them.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sunan Abu Dawud and al-Bayhaqi narrations forbidding the killing of bats; methodological consensus across Hanafi, Shafi'i, and Hanbali.

ingredients.name: rat meat
ingredients.reason: Rats are among the vermin (fawasiq) excluded from lawful animals.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Bukhari 3314 (vermin explicitly named); methodological consensus.

ingredients.name: mouse meat
ingredients.reason: Mice are among the vermin (fawasiq) excluded from lawful animals.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Bukhari 3314 (vermin explicitly named); methodological consensus.

ingredients.name: scorpion
ingredients.reason: Venomous animal classed among the vermin explicitly named for killing on sight; not among lawful foods.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Bukhari 1828, Sahih Muslim 1198 (Prophet permitted killing scorpions among the vermin); methodological consensus.

ingredients.name: centipede
ingredients.reason: Falls under the prohibited insect / vermin category in the majority view; venomous arthropods are doubly excluded.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across Hanafi, Shafi'i, Hanbali.

ingredients.name: spider
ingredients.reason: Venomous arthropod; not among lawful animal categories.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus across Hanafi, Shafi'i, Hanbali.

ingredients.name: monkey meat
ingredients.reason: Primates are classed among prohibited animals — possessing fangs and grouped with the prohibited beasts in classical jurisprudence.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Muslim 1933 (prohibition of every fanged beast). Methodological consensus.

ingredients.name: weasel meat
ingredients.reason: Fanged carnivore; falls under the general prohibition of beasts of prey.

ingredients.name: ferret meat
ingredients.reason: Mustelid carnivore with fangs; prohibited as a beast of prey.

ingredients.name: otter meat
ingredients.reason: Carnivorous mustelid with fangs; prohibited as a beast of prey per the majority. (A minority opinion treats sea otter under sea-creature rules; default reflects the majority.)
ingredients.sourceNotes: Sahih Muslim 1933. Methodological consensus across Hanafi, Shafi'i, Hanbali.

ingredients.name: raccoon meat
ingredients.reason: Predatory omnivore with fangs and claws; classed with prohibited beasts of prey.

ingredients.name: mongoose meat
ingredients.reason: Fanged carnivore; prohibited as a beast of prey.

ingredients.name: blood sausage
ingredients.reason: Made primarily from coagulated blood, which is explicitly prohibited.

ingredients.name: wine sauce
ingredients.reason: Sauce built on wine; the underlying intoxicant retains its haram classification.

ingredients.name: brandy butter
ingredients.reason: Sweetened compound containing distilled spirit as a primary flavoring; the alcohol fraction is unmasked.

ingredients.name: liquor-filled chocolate
ingredients.reason: Chocolate filled with whole liqueur or spirit; the alcohol fraction is intentional and unmasked.

ingredients.name: meat extract
ingredients.reason: Source animal and slaughter rarely indicated; common base for soup and savory products.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-origin ingredient defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: beef extract
ingredients.reason: Concentrated beef stock; inherits the zabiha-slaughter question.

ingredients.name: chicken extract
ingredients.reason: Concentrated poultry stock; inherits the zabiha-slaughter question.

ingredients.name: liver extract
ingredients.reason: Source animal and slaughter rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-origin ingredient defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: meat flavoring
ingredients.reason: Generic label gives no information on source animal or slaughter method.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-origin flavoring defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: beef flavoring
ingredients.reason: Beef flavorings often built on beef extract; inherits the zabiha-slaughter question.

ingredients.name: chicken flavoring
ingredients.reason: Chicken flavorings often built on chicken extract; inherits the zabiha-slaughter question.

ingredients.name: pâté
ingredients.reason: Liver-and-fat spread; source animal and slaughter rarely indicated. Some pâtés also include brandy or wine.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal source and possible alcohol carrier both default to doubtful.

ingredients.name: terrine
ingredients.reason: Coarse meat-and-fat preparation similar to pâté; same animal-source and possible alcohol concerns.

ingredients.name: hot dog
ingredients.reason: Hot dogs may be pork-, beef-, chicken-, or turkey-based; uncertified products rarely indicate source or slaughter, and natural casings are typically pork or sheep intestine.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified meat source and casing default to doubtful.

ingredients.name: sausage
ingredients.reason: Generic 'sausage' covers pork, beef, poultry, and mixed-meat formulations; casings may be animal-derived. Origin and slaughter rarely indicated.

ingredients.name: knockwurst
ingredients.reason: German short sausage typically pork-and-beef but sometimes labeled without species; also commonly pork-cased.

ingredients.name: bratwurst
ingredients.reason: Traditionally pork; some formulations are veal or beef. Without explicit species labeling, defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: salami
ingredients.reason: Most commercial salami is pork; beef and turkey salami exist. Without explicit species labeling, defaults to doubtful (pork salami is haram).

ingredients.name: corned beef
ingredients.reason: Beef inherits the zabiha-slaughter question. The cure (sodium nitrite, salt) is itself halal.

ingredients.name: pastrami
ingredients.reason: Cured-and-smoked beef or turkey; inherits the zabiha-slaughter question.

ingredients.name: jerky
ingredients.reason: Dried-and-cured meat; inherits the zabiha-slaughter question. Marinades sometimes include wine or soy sauce of unverified origin.

ingredients.name: ground meat
ingredients.reason: Inherits the zabiha-slaughter question of the source animal. Mixed-species grinds add ambiguity.

ingredients.name: meatballs
ingredients.reason: Inherits the slaughter question of the source meat; commercial formulations may also include broth or natural flavors of unverified origin.

ingredients.name: processed meat
ingredients.reason: Umbrella term covering pork, beef, and poultry products; without species and slaughter labels, defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: hydrogenated fat
ingredients.reason: Hydrogenation is a process, not a source. Vegetable oil hydrogenated is halal; animal fat hydrogenated retains the source-animal slaughter question. Source is rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fat source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: margarine
ingredients.reason: Predominantly vegetable-fat-based today, but commercial margarine often includes mono- and diglycerides, lecithin of unverified source, or whey/milk derivatives that inherit upstream questions.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: composite product with unspecified emulsifier and dairy-derivative sources defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: butter flavor
ingredients.reason: Often delivered in an ethanol or propylene glycol carrier; specific source rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified flavoring carrier defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: almond extract
ingredients.reason: Standard almond extract is prepared in an ethanol carrier (typically 35%+ alcohol). Same scholarly disagreement as vanilla extract.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful. Alcohol-free almond flavoring, when explicitly labeled, is halal.

ingredients.name: peppermint extract
ingredients.reason: Commonly delivered in an ethanol carrier. Same scholarly disagreement as vanilla extract.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful. Oil-based peppermint flavoring is halal.

ingredients.name: lemon extract
ingredients.reason: Typically prepared in an ethanol carrier. Same scholarly disagreement as vanilla extract.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful. Lemon oil and zest are halal.

ingredients.name: orange extract
ingredients.reason: Typically prepared in an ethanol carrier.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: cherry flavoring
ingredients.reason: Commonly delivered in an ethanol carrier; maraschino-style flavorings derive from cherry liqueur.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on alcohol carriers; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: coffee extract
ingredients.reason: Often prepared in an ethanol carrier. Brewed coffee and instant coffee themselves are halal.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on alcohol carriers; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: butterscotch flavoring
ingredients.reason: Often delivered in an ethanol or propylene glycol carrier and may include butter-flavor compounds of unverified source.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified flavoring carrier defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: smoke flavor
ingredients.reason: Liquid smoke is typically water-based and halal in principle, but commercial formulations are sometimes carried in ethanol or paired with unspecified natural flavors.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified carrier and flavor blend default to doubtful.

ingredients.name: malt extract
ingredients.reason: Concentrated wort from sprouted barley. The brewing-adjacent process can leave residual ethanol; whether the trace amount is halal is disputed.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on residual ethanol; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: flavoring
ingredients.reason: Bare 'flavoring' / 'flavor' label is non-specific and may include alcohol carriers, animal-derived components, or castoreum.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified flavoring defaults to doubtful (US FDA 21 CFR 101.22 permits non-specific flavor labels).

ingredients.name: artificial flavor
ingredients.reason: Synthetic origin does not preclude an ethanol carrier or animal-derived component; the regulatory term doesn't pin down composition.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified composition defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: rice vinegar
ingredients.reason: Some rice vinegars are produced from sake (rice wine); others are direct rice-fermentation vinegars. Without the production route on the label, defaults to doubtful.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus mirroring the wine-vinegar treatment.

ingredients.name: sherry vinegar
ingredients.reason: Produced by acetic-acid fermentation of sherry (a fortified wine). Same transformation / istihalah disagreement as wine vinegar.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: krill
ingredients.reason: Crustacean, similar in classification to shrimp. Same Hanafi-vs-three-schools disagreement on non-scaled sea creatures.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: krill oil
ingredients.reason: Inherits the school-level disagreement on krill as a non-scaled crustacean.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: sea cucumber
ingredients.reason: Echinoderm, not a scaled fish. Same Hanafi-vs-three-schools disagreement as other non-scaled sea creatures.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: jellyfish
ingredients.reason: Cnidarian, no scales. Same Hanafi-vs-three-schools disagreement as other non-scaled sea creatures.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: abalone
ingredients.reason: Marine gastropod mollusk. Same Hanafi-vs-three-schools disagreement on non-scaled sea creatures.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: sea urchin
ingredients.reason: Echinoderm, no scales. Same Hanafi-vs-three-schools disagreement.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented school-level disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: lanolin
ingredients.reason: Wax secreted by sheep wool follicles, recovered without slaughtering the animal. Some authorities permit it (no slaughter required for the secretion); others treat any sheep-derived ingredient as inheriting the slaughter question. Lanolin-derived vitamin D3 in supplements is the most common food-adjacent appearance.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on non-slaughter animal byproducts; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: vitamin d3
ingredients.reason: Most commercial vitamin D3 is extracted from lanolin; algae-sourced (vegan) D3 is increasingly available but rarely labeled. Source typically not indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: lanolin-derived D3 inherits the lanolin disagreement; defaults to doubtful when source is unstated. Algae-sourced D3 and D2 (ergocalciferol) are halal.

ingredients.name: coenzyme q10
ingredients.reason: Commercially produced via yeast or bacterial fermentation, but some routes use beef-heart extracts. Source rarely indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified production route defaults to doubtful. Fermentation-derived CoQ10 is halal.

ingredients.name: l-carnitine
ingredients.reason: Originally extracted from animal muscle (Latin 'carnis' = flesh). Modern synthetic and fermentation-derived L-carnitine exist; source not always indicated.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: bone meal
ingredients.reason: Ground animal bone used as a calcium / mineral source; inherits the slaughter question of the source animal.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified animal-source ingredient defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: bone char
ingredients.reason: Charred animal bone historically used to filter cane sugar and as a black pigment. Inherits the slaughter question of the source animal. Vegetable carbon (E153) is the alternative.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on whether the carbonization process (transformation) renders the result halal; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: fatty acids
ingredients.reason: Generic 'fatty acids' label gives no information on plant or animal origin.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fatty-acid source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: oleic acid
ingredients.reason: Can be derived from animal fat (tallow) or vegetable oil (olive, sunflower). Source rarely specified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fatty-acid source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: palmitic acid
ingredients.reason: Can be derived from animal fat or palm oil. Source rarely specified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fatty-acid source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: myristic acid
ingredients.reason: Can be derived from animal fat or vegetable oil (coconut, nutmeg). Source rarely specified.

ingredients.name: cetyl alcohol
ingredients.reason: Historically derived from whale spermaceti; now usually plant-based or synthetic, but source rarely specified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified source defaults to doubtful. Plant-derived or synthetic cetyl alcohol is halal.

ingredients.name: stearyl alcohol
ingredients.reason: Can be plant-, synthetic-, or tallow-derived. Source rarely specified.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified source defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: cetearyl alcohol
ingredients.reason: Blend of cetyl and stearyl alcohols; same source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: calcium stearate
ingredients.reason: Stearic-acid salt; inherits the animal-vs-vegetable source ambiguity of stearic acid.

ingredients.name: zinc stearate
ingredients.reason: Stearic-acid salt; inherits the animal-vs-vegetable source ambiguity of stearic acid.

ingredients.name: potassium stearate
ingredients.reason: Stearic-acid salt; inherits the source ambiguity of stearic acid.

ingredients.name: nougat
ingredients.reason: Traditional nougat uses egg whites and honey; modern commercial nougat often substitutes gelatin and may include alcohol-based flavorings.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified gelatin and possible flavor carrier default to doubtful.

ingredients.name: licorice
ingredients.reason: Licorice root itself is halal; commercial licorice candy commonly includes gelatin or beeswax glaze and sometimes alcohol-based flavorings.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus on licorice candy as a composite; the root extract on its own is halal.

ingredients.name: fondant
ingredients.reason: Sugar-based icing; commercial formulations may include gelatin, glycerin, and flavorings of unverified source.

ingredients.name: gummy candy
ingredients.reason: Standard gummy candies use gelatin of unspecified animal origin. Pectin- or agar-based gummies are halal.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: shares unspecified gelatin's source ambiguity.

ingredients.name: ice cream
ingredients.reason: Composite product. Cream is halal; commercial ice cream commonly includes mono- and diglycerides, vanilla extract, and natural flavors that inherit upstream questions.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus on composite products with unspecified-origin emulsifiers and flavors.

ingredients.name: whipped topping
ingredients.reason: Often built on hydrogenated vegetable oil with mono- and diglycerides, polysorbate 60, and sodium caseinate of unverified upstream origin.

ingredients.name: non-dairy creamer
ingredients.reason: 'Non-dairy' is a US labeling term that still permits sodium caseinate (a milk derivative). Includes mono- and diglycerides and natural flavors of unverified origin.

ingredients.name: chewing gum
ingredients.reason: Modern gum bases are typically synthetic polymers but may also include glycerin, lanolin-derived softeners, or stearic acid of unspecified source.

ingredients.name: lard substitute
ingredients.reason: Generally hydrogenated vegetable fat, but the label is non-specific and some formulations include animal fat.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Methodological consensus: unspecified fat composition defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: cream of tartar
ingredients.reason: By-product of wine-making (tartaric crystals from wine barrels). Inherits the wine-derived disagreement that applies to tartaric acid.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement on wine-derived tartrates; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: sodium tartrate
ingredients.reason: Tartrate salt; inherits the wine-derived ambiguity of tartaric acid.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful.

ingredients.name: rochelle salt
ingredients.reason: Mixed sodium-potassium tartrate; inherits the wine-derived ambiguity of tartaric acid.
ingredients.sourceNotes: Documented scholarly disagreement; defaults to doubtful.
